channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The private sector is rolling over for Donald Trump
item.description: Donald Trump didn’t win the 2024 election in a landslide, but that hasn’t stopped him from claiming an electoral mandate. If that ever held water, it doesn’t anymore. With the stock market plunging from ill-advised global tariffs, Trump’s public approval has already sunk into the negatives.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Kashmir's Sufi music lovers are sticking with the audio cassette
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Farooq Ahmad Shaksaaz presses a button on his 1970 Sharp cassette player, and with a hefty clack the machine whirrs to life.
item.dc:creator: DAR YASIN and AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cybersecurity officials warn against potentially costly Medusa ransomware attacks
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The FBI and the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency are warning against a dangerous ransomware scheme.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: This free web-based image editor gives Photoshop a run for its money
item.description: AI image editing may be all the rage, but good old-fashioned image editors are still essential. There’s a problem, though: Windows PCs, Chromebooks, and Macs don’t include exceptional image editors that go beyond the most basic editing needs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: Driverless 'bus of the future' is tested in Barcelona
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Commuters in downtown Barcelona have been able to ride the bus for free this week. There’s just one catch: this mini-bus has no one at the wheel.
item.dc:creator: HERNAN MUÑOZ and JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Critics warn staff cuts at federal agencies overseeing US dams could put public safety at risk
item.description: Experts are warning that Trump administration workforce cuts at federal agencies overseeing U.S. dams are threatening their ability to provide reliable electricity, supply water to farmers and protect communities from floods.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: You can now watch a full Metallica concert on Apple Vision Pro
item.description: You don’t need tickets to see Metallica in concert anymore—just a $3,499 Apple Vision Pro headset. Starting today, Apple Vision Pro users can experience Metallica’s 2024 Mexico City concert as an immersive, ultrahigh resolution experience complete with 180-degree video and Spatial Audio.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Vance hopeful 'high-level' TikTok deal will be completed by early April
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Friday that he was hopeful a deal to keep TikTok operating in the U.S. will be wrapped up by the early April deadline.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Got a road toll text? Don’t click on this ‘smishing’ scam
item.description: State officials are warning Americans not to respond to a surge of scam road toll collection texts. The texts impersonating state road toll collection agencies attempt to get phone users to reveal financial information, such as credit or debit cards or bank accounts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Refocusing CISA under Trump: A strategic approach to critical infrastructure defense
item.description: Refocusing CISA's efforts promises to defend against current threats while anticipating and mitigating future challenges, securing America's cyber future. The post Refocusing CISA under Trump: A strategic approach to critical infrastructure defense first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Block Party offers tool for optimizing social media privacy
item.description: If optimizing your social media privacy settings feels important but overwhelming, a company called Block Party may be able to help. Founder and CEO Tracy Chou has long been known for advocating for diversity in the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Stupendous Python stunts without a net
item.description: In this week’s Python Report: Sly tricks for setting up Python on a machine with no network, snappier Python code with asyncand await, swifter Python programs with Zig, and slicker web apps with HTMX and Django.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: You can now use an IPv4 address as business collateral - and it could be worth millions
item.description: You can now secure a loan using your IPv4 address as collateral – you’ll even still be able to access and use it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Android 16 Beta 3 has arrived – here are the 4 features I think will be the most useful
item.description: Android 16 Beta 3 has arrived, bringing with it a handful of useful new features that aim to improve accessibility, make it easier to monitor the longevity of your phone, and keep local networks secure.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Apple will finally enable encrypted RCS messages between iOS and Android, and it's about time
item.description: Apple will, at long last, enabling end-to-end encryption for RCS messages, allowing iPhones and Android phones to text each other with the security encryption provides for the first time.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Women in tech statistics: The hard truths of an uphill battle
item.description: Diversity is critical to IT performance. Diverse teams perform better, hire better talent, have more engaged members, and retain workers better than those that don’t focus on diversity and inclusion, according to a recent report from McKinsey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Intel under Tan: What enterprise IT buyers need to know
item.description: Intel’s appointment of semiconductor veteran Lip-Bu Tan as CEO marks a critical moment for the company and its enterprise customers. With rising competition from AMD, Arm-based chips, and RISC-V alternatives, Intel faces mounting pressure to defend its x86 dominance.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The future of AI isn’t the model—it’s the system
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Arbitrator sides with Meta in bid to halt sales of ex-employee's memoir
item.description: An arbitrator has sided with Meta in its case against a former employee who made a series of misconduct allegations about the Facebook and Instagram parent company in a memoir published this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cisco’s chief product officer explains the company’s transformation around AI
item.description: For 40 years, Cisco has been best known for building routers, switches, and other networking technology that connects computers within offices, data centers, and across the internet.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: New tools from OpenAI help companies create their own AI agents
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday launched Responses, a new api intended to eventually replace Assistants. The big draw? Responses provides a number of new tools that companies and organizations can use to create their own AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trade groups urge the administration to maintain basic research funding
item.description: "The Trump administration's efforts to reduce the size of the federal government have resulted in decreased funding for biomedical research," said Sandra Babosu The post Trade groups urge the administration to maintain basic research funding first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
